title: The Boy Who Cried Wolf
lines: A Boy who watched the sheep was in the habit of crying, 'Wolf! Wolf!' when there was no wolf.
The villagers came running to help him, but found that the alarm was false.
He did this several times, and every time the Wolf! Wolf! brought the people running.
But one day a real wolf came and began to worry the sheep.
The Boy cried out in genuine fear, 'Wolf! Wolf!'
But this time the villagers, supposing him to be deceiving them as before, did not come to his aid.
The wolf killed many of the sheep.
moral: A liar will not be believed, even when he tells the truth.
